issues.title: Coverage: Configuration: code tables (list, get, update)
issues.body_raw:
**Domain:** Configuration
**Priority:** High
**Effort:** M (1–3 days)

## Background

Add support for the Alma Configuration API code-tables endpoints. Code tables are institution-wide config (rare updates, admin-only); the PUT replaces the entire table, so the typical workflow is pull → mutate → push.

## Relevant Alma API documentation

- Alma dev-network: https://developers.exlibrisgroup.com/alma/apis/conf/
- Endpoints touched:
  - `GET /almaws/v1/conf/code-tables`
  - `GET /almaws/v1/conf/code-tables/{codeTableName}`
  - `PUT /almaws/v1/conf/code-tables/{codeTableName}`

**Audit finding (2026-05-01):** Alma docs do NOT document a `scope` query parameter on `GET /almaws/v1/conf/code-tables`. The original signature `list_code_tables(self, scope: str = None)` was inferred — it should either be dropped or documented as a documented filter is found.

## Current wrapper behavior

- No code-tables support in `Configuration` domain class.

## Expected wrapper behavior

```python
def list_code_tables(self) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]
def get_code_table(self, code_table_name: str) -> Dict[str, Any]
def update_code_table(self, code_table_name: str, code_table_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> AlmaResponse
```

If a future API revision adds a documented filter for `list_code_tables`, expose it then via `**kwargs` pass-through. Do NOT ship the undocumented `scope` parameter.

## Implementation notes

- Files to touch:
  - `src/almaapitk/domains/configuration.py`
  - `tests/unit/domains/test_configuration.py`
- Mirror an existing domain method as the pattern source (e.g., `Acquisitions.get_invoice` for read).
- All public methods must have type hints + Google-style docstrings.
- Validate required inputs at top of method and raise `AlmaValidationError` with a clear message.
- Log method entry with key parameters (info), success with result identifiers (info), and errors with full context (error).
- NEVER print — use `self.logger`.
- Code tables are institution-wide config; updates are usually rare and require admin permissions in Alma.
- The PUT replaces the entire table — pull, mutate, push.
- Add a helper `update_code_table_row(table, code, description)` later if user demand justifies it (out of scope for this issue).

<!-- prereqs:auto:begin -->
## Prerequisites

**Hard blockers — must merge before this issue is ready to start:**
- #22 — Configuration domain class bootstrap

**Recommended (would simplify or improve this work, but not strictly blocking):**
- #3 — Persistent requests.Session (every new HTTP call inherits connection pooling)
- #4 — Consolidate HTTP verbs into _request() (every new method routes through one chokepoint)
- #5 — Retry with exponential backoff for 429 / 5xx (every new method gets 429/5xx resilience for free)
- #9 — Map Alma error codes to specific exception subclasses (new methods raise typed Alma errors instead of generic AlmaAPIError)
- #10 — Propagate tracking_id and alma_code on errors (errors carry tracking_id for support escalation)
- #14 — Replace print() with logger; remove safe_request() (no print() statements in the new methods)
- #11 — iter_paged() generator at the client level (list/search methods should use iter_paged() instead of manual offset loops)

<!-- prereqs:auto:end -->

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] All listed methods implemented on the existing domain class with type hints and docstrings.
- [ ] `list_code_tables()` takes no `scope` parameter (or any other undocumented filter).
- [ ] Each method has unit-test coverage (mocked HTTP) and at least one integration test against SANDBOX.
- [ ] Errors raise `AlmaValidationError` (input) or `AlmaAPIError` / subclass (API).
- [ ] All operations logged through `alma_logging` (no `print`).
- [ ] `scripts/smoke_import.py` and `tests/test_public_api_contract.py` still pass.
- [ ] If new public symbols are exposed, they are added to `almaapitk/__init__.py` `__all__` and the lazy import map.

## Open questions

- If at implementation time the docs do list a query filter (e.g. `scope`/`type`/`area`) on `GET /code-tables`, expose it as a typed kwarg. Otherwise ship without filters.

## Audit notes (2026-05-01)

- Audit flagged that the proposed `scope` query parameter is not documented on the list endpoint. AC now explicitly forbids shipping it without doc evidence.

---
Filed as part of the coverage-expansion backlog. See `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-30-coverage-expansion-design.md` for context.
acceptance_criteria: All listed methods implemented on the existing domain class with type hints and docstrings.
`list_code_tables()` takes no `scope` parameter (or any other undocumented filter).
Each method has unit-test coverage (mocked HTTP) and at least one integration test against SANDBOX.
If new public symbols are exposed, they are added to `almaapitk/__init__.py` `__all__` and the lazy import map.

issues.title: Coverage: Configuration: mapping tables (list, get, update)
issues.body_raw:
**Domain:** Configuration
**Priority:** High
**Effort:** M (1–3 days)

## API endpoints touched
- `GET /almaws/v1/conf/mapping-tables`
- `GET /almaws/v1/conf/mapping-tables/{mappingTableName}`
- `PUT /almaws/v1/conf/mapping-tables/{mappingTableName}`

## Methods to add
```python
def list_mapping_tables(self) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]
def get_mapping_table(self, mapping_table_name: str) -> Dict[str, Any]
def update_mapping_table(self, mapping_table_name: str, mapping_table_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> AlmaResponse
```

## Files to touch
- `src/almaapitk/domains/configuration.py`
- `tests/unit/domains/test_configuration.py`

## References
- Alma dev-network: https://developers.exlibrisgroup.com/alma/apis/conf/
- Use the `alma-api-expert` skill for endpoint quirks and validation rules

<!-- prereqs:auto:begin -->
## Prerequisites

**Hard blockers — must merge before this issue is ready to start:**
- #22 — Configuration domain class bootstrap

**Recommended (would simplify or improve this work, but not strictly blocking):**
- #3 — Persistent requests.Session (every new HTTP call inherits connection pooling)
- #4 — Consolidate HTTP verbs into _request() (every new method routes through one chokepoint)
- #5 — Retry with exponential backoff for 429 / 5xx (every new method gets 429/5xx resilience for free)
- #9 — Map Alma error codes to specific exception subclasses (new methods raise typed Alma errors instead of generic AlmaAPIError)
- #10 — Propagate tracking_id and alma_code on errors (errors carry tracking_id for support escalation)
- #14 — Replace print() with logger; remove safe_request() (no print() statements in the new methods)
- #11 — iter_paged() generator at the client level (list/search methods should use iter_paged() instead of manual offset loops)

<!-- prereqs:auto:end -->

## Acceptance criteria
- All listed methods implemented on the existing domain class with type hints and docstrings.
- Each method has unit-test coverage (mocked HTTP) and at least one integration test against SANDBOX.
- Errors raise `AlmaValidationError` (input) or `AlmaAPIError` / subclass (API).
- All operations logged through `alma_logging` (no `print`).
- `scripts/smoke_import.py` and `tests/test_public_api_contract.py` still pass.
- If new public symbols are exposed, they are added to `almaapitk/__init__.py` `__all__` and the lazy import map.

## Notes for the implementing agent
- Mirror an existing domain method as the pattern source (e.g., `Acquisitions.get_invoice` for read, `Acquisitions.create_invoice_simple` for write).
- All public methods must have type hints + Google-style docstrings.
- Validate required inputs at top of method and raise `AlmaValidationError` with a clear message.
- Log method entry with key parameters (info), success with result identifiers (info), and errors with full context (error).
- NEVER print — use `self.logger`.
- Test in SANDBOX first. Add at least one unit test under `tests/unit/` and one integration test under `tests/integration/`.
- After implementation, `poetry run python scripts/smoke_import.py` and the public-API contract test MUST pass.

- Identical shape to code tables (#5) — copy that implementation pattern.
- Mapping tables can grow large; ensure the PUT roundtrip handles them.

---
Filed as part of the coverage-expansion backlog. See `docs/superpowers/specs/2026-04-30-coverage-expansion-design.md` for context.
